---
title: "Get started with rsgl"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Get started with rsgl}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(rsgl)
```

## What is SGL?

*Placeholder.* A short pitch for SGL — a grammar of graphics language designed
to look and feel like SQL — and how rsgl translates SGL statements against any
DBI connection into a `ggplot2` plot.

## Installation

*Placeholder.* Install instructions (CRAN once published, dev version from
GitHub).

## Your first plot

*Placeholder.* End-to-end example: open a DBI connection, write data, call
`dbGetPlot()` with an SGL statement.

## Where to next

*Placeholder.* Pointers to the example gallery and the SGL language guide.
